body.u: As I look back over my life I think first of all about when I was leaving school. I can remember wanting to leave school but I also wanted to stay on and learn shorthand and typing.

I had seen the careers officer about a career in a library or with children. Working with children meant going to college and I knew I didn’t want to do that so I found out about librarianship, as many jobs weren’t around in that or so it seemed I decided to stay on and try and gain some extra exams.

At the time of leaveingleaving school I had a part time job working at a plant nursery which I enjoyed very much. The man who I worked for let me work there full time until September when I went back to school.
When I left the sixth year I applied for secretarial jobs one of which I got. This job did not involve promotion although I would have liked it to.
Looking back I wonder why I took that job because it got boring. It was the same thing nearly every day.
I would have liked a library career much better because I could have gone to different libraries to work and the routine would have changed.

Eventually after a couple of years at that job I got married and left work when my children grew up I went back to work as a personal secretary this job wasn’t boring as I was working for my husband and we did a lot of travelling I saw places I had never seen before.

When I retired I missed all the travelling around but I have my memioriesmemories and my grandchildren are always asking for stories when we went travelling. My husband is still with me and we are still happy my life turned out fine after all.
